questions.question_text: Why is our portfolio NOI $4.2M higher than what auditors are calculating?
questions.answer_summary: Three properties use gross lease accounting while the consolidated model assumes net leases. Operating expense pass-throughs are counted as revenue in one system and netted out in another, inflating NOI by 11%.

questions.question_text: Is our vacancy rate actually 6% or are we masking move-out timing?
questions.answer_summary: The leasing dashboard marks units as occupied until lease expiration, not actual move-out. Physical vacancy is 9.3% when accounting for 47 units that are empty but still under lease.

questions.question_text: How accurate are the cap rates we're showing investors in our quarterly deck?
questions.answer_summary: Cap rate calculations use trailing NOI but forward-looking expense assumptions. Standardizing the time horizon shifts portfolio valuation down by $18M across 12 properties.

questions.question_text: Are we reserving enough for deferred maintenance across the portfolio?
questions.answer_summary: Maintenance reserve models haven't incorporated the 2024 capital expenditure actuals. Four properties are under-reserved by a combined $1.8M based on updated replacement schedules.

questions.question_text: What's really driving our tenant retention number down this quarter?
questions.answer_summary: The retention metric counts lease renewals but excludes month-to-month conversions. Including holdover tenants who haven't formally renewed raises retention from 72% to 81%, changing the narrative entirely.
